heading: Requirements for sponsor’s affidavit of support
label: 1183a Requirements for sponsor’s affidavit of support
notes: References in TextSubsection (e), referred to in subsec. (a)(1)(B), does not define “means-tested public benefit”.The Social Security Act, referred to in subsec. (a)(3)(A), (B), is , . L. 111–83“(i) the individual petitioning under for the classification of such alien died after the approval of such petition; andsection 1154 of this title“(ii) the Attorney General has determined for humanitarian reasons that revocation of such petition under would be inappropriate.”section 1155 of this title2002—Subsec. (f)(2), (4)(B)(ii). , substituted “paragraph (5)(A)” for “paragraph (5)”.Pub. L. 107–150, § 2(a)(3)Subsec. (f)(5). , amended heading and text of par. (5) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Such term also includes an individual who does not meet the requirement of paragraph (1)(D) but who accepts joint and several liability with a petitioning sponsor under paragraph (2) or relative of an employment-based immigrant under paragraph (4) and who demonstrates (as provided under paragraph (6)) the means to maintain an annual income equal to at least 125 percent of the Federal poverty line.”Pub. C, title V, § 551(c)110 Stat. 3009–679Sept. 30, 1996“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [enacting this section, amending sections 1631 and 1632 of this title, and repealing provisions set out as a note under this section] shall apply to affidavits of support executed on or after a date specified by the Attorney General, which date shall be not earlier than 60 days (and not later than 90 days) after the date the Attorney General formulates the form for such affidavits under paragraph (2).“(2).—Promulgation of formNot later than 90 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Attorney General, in consultation with the heads of other appropriate agencies, shall promulgate a standard form for an affidavit of support consistent with the provisions of section 213A of the Immigration and Nationality Act [this section], as amended by subsection (a).”Sept. 30, 1996, , , provided that subsec. (a) of this section was applicable to affidavits of support executed on or after a date specified by Attorney General, which date was to be not earlier than 60 days (and not later than 90 days) after date Attorney General formulated form for such affidavits under subsec. (b) of this section, prior to repeal by , , .Pub. L. 104–193, title IV, § 423(c)110 Stat. 2273Pub. L. 104–208, div. C, title V, § 551(b)(2)104 Stat. 3009–679Aug. 22, 1996Sept. 30, 1996

Fees Relating to Affidavits of Support, , , 1501A–425, as amended by , , ; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 106–113, div. B, § 1000(a)(7) [div. A, title II, § 232]113 Stat. 1536Pub. L. 107–228, div. A, title II, § 211(b)116 Stat. 1365Pub. L. 115–31, div. J, title VII, § 7081(e)131 Stat. 716Nov. 29, 1999Sept. 30, 2002May 5, 2017“(a).—Authority To Charge FeeThe Secretary of State may charge and retain a fee or surcharge for services provided by the Department of State to any sponsor who provides an affidavit of support under section 213A of the Immigration and Nationality Act () to ensure that such affidavit is properly completed before it is forwarded to a consular post for adjudication by a consular officer in connection with the adjudication of an immigrant visa. Such fee or surcharge shall be in addition to and separate from any fee imposed for immigrant visa application processing and issuance, and shall recover only the costs of such services not recovered by such fee.8 U.S.C. 1183a“(b).—LimitationAny fee established under subsection (a) shall be charged only once to a sponsor or joint sponsors who file essentially duplicative affidavits of support in connection with separate immigrant visa applications from the spouse and children of any petitioner required by the Immigration and Nationality Act [ et seq.] to petition separately for such persons.8 U.S.C. 1101“(c).—Treatment of FeesFees collected under the authority of subsection (a) shall be deposited in the Consular and Border Security Programs account to recover the cost of providing consular services. Such fees shall remain available for obligation until expended.”

L. 106–78, title VII, § 752(b)(6)113 Stat. 1169Aug. 22, 1996Oct. 21, 1998Oct. 22, 1999“Requirements for reimbursement by a sponsor for benefits provided to a sponsored alien pursuant to an affidavit of support under section 213A of the Immigration and Nationality Act [] shall not apply with respect to the following:8 U.S.C. 1183a“(1)Medical assistance described in section 401(b)(1)(A) [] or assistance described in section 411(b)(1) [].8 U.S.C. 1611(b)(1)(A)8 U.S.C. 1621(b)(1)“(2)Short-term, non-cash, in-kind emergency disaster relief.“(3)Assistance or benefits under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 1751“(4)Assistance or benefits under the Child Nutrition Act of 1966 [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 1771“(5)Public health assistance for immunizations (not including any assistance under title XIX of the Social Security Act [ et seq.]) with respect to immunizable diseases and for testing and treatment of symptoms of communicable diseases whether or not such symptoms are caused by a communicable disease.42 U.S.C. 1396“(6)Payments for foster care and adoption assistance under parts B and E of title IV of the Social Security Act [ et seq., 670 et seq.] for a parent or a child, but only if the foster or adoptive parent (or parents) of such child is a qualified alien (as defined in section 431 []).42 U.S.C. 6208 U.S.C. 1641“(7)Programs, services, or assistance (such as soup kitchens, crisis counseling and intervention, and short-term shelter) specified by the Attorney General, in the Attorney General’s sole and unreviewable discretion after consultation with appropriate Federal agencies and departments, which (A) deliver in-kind services at the community level, including through public or private nonprofit agencies; (B) do not condition the provision of assistance, the amount of assistance provided, or the cost of assistance provided on the individual recipient’s income or resources; and (C) are necessary for the protection of life or safety.“(8)Programs of student assistance under titles IV, V, IX, and X of the Higher Education Act of 1965 [ et seq., 1101 et seq., 1134 et seq., 1135 et seq.], and titles III, VII, and VIII of the Public Health Service Act [ et seq., 292 et seq., 296 et seq.].20 U.S.C. 107042 U.S.C. 241“(9)Benefits under the Head Start Act [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 9831“(10)Means-tested programs under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 [ et seq.].20 U.S.C. 6301“(11)Benefits under the [sic] title I of the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 [former et seq.].”29 U.S.C. 2801

children.label: A
children.text: in which the sponsor agrees to provide support to maintain the sponsored alien at an annual income that is not less than 125 percent of the Federal poverty line during the period in which the affidavit is enforceable;

children.label: B
children.text: that is legally enforceable against the sponsor by the sponsored alien, the Federal Government, any State (or any political subdivision of such State), or by any other entity that provides any means-tested public benefit (as defined in subsection (e) ), consistent with the provisions of this section; and1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: C
children.text: in which the sponsor agrees to submit to the jurisdiction of any Federal or State court for the purpose of actions brought under subsection (b)(2).

children.heading: Period of enforceability
children.label: 2 Period of enforceability
children.text: An affidavit of support shall be enforceable with respect to benefits provided for an alien before the date the alien is naturalized as a citizen of the United States, or, if earlier, the termination date provided under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Termination of period of enforceability upon completion of required period of employment, etc.
children.label: 3 Termination of period of enforceability upon completion of required period of employment, etc.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An affidavit of support is not enforceable after such time as the alien (i) has worked 40 qualifying quarters of coverage as defined under title II of the Social Security Act [ et seq.] or can be credited with such qualifying quarters as provided under subparagraph (B), and (ii) in the case of any such qualifying quarter creditable for any period beginning after , did not receive any Federal means-tested public benefit (as provided under ) during any such period.42 U.S.C. 401section 1613 of this titleDecember 31, 1996

children.label: i
children.text: all of the qualifying quarters of coverage as defined under title II of the Social Security Act worked by a parent of such alien while the alien was under age 18, and

children.label: ii
children.text: all of the qualifying quarters worked by a spouse of such alien during their marriage and the alien remains married to such spouse or such spouse is deceased.

children.heading: Provision of information to save system
children.label: C Provision of information to save system
children.text: The Attorney General shall ensure that appropriate information regarding the application of this paragraph is provided to the system for alien verification of eligibility (SAVE) described in section 1137(d)(3) of the Social Security Act [].42 U.S.C. 1320b–7(d)(3)

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: A Requirement
children.text: Upon notification that a sponsored alien has received any means-tested public benefit, the appropriate nongovernmental entity which provided such benefit or the appropriate entity of the Federal Government, a State, or any political subdivision of a State shall request reimbursement by the sponsor in an amount which is equal to the unreimbursed costs of such benefit.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: B Regulations
children.text: The Attorney General, in consultation with the heads of other appropriate Federal agencies, shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out subparagraph (A).

children.heading: In case of nonresponse
children.label: A In case of nonresponse
children.text: If within 45 days after a request for reimbursement under paragraph (1)(A), the appropriate entity has not received a response from the sponsor indicating a willingness to commence payment an action may be brought against the sponsor pursuant to the affidavit of support.

children.heading: In case of failure to pay
children.label: B In case of failure to pay
children.text: If the sponsor fails to abide by the repayment terms established by the appropriate entity, the entity may bring an action against the sponsor pursuant to the affidavit of support.

children.heading: Limitation on actions
children.label: C Limitation on actions
children.text: No cause of action may be brought under this paragraph later than 10 years after the date on which the sponsored alien last received any means-tested public benefit to which the affidavit of support applies.

children.heading: Use of collection agencies
children.label: 3 Use of collection agencies
children.text: If the appropriate entity under paragraph (1)(A) requests reimbursement from the sponsor or brings an action against the sponsor pursuant to the affidavit of support, the appropriate entity may appoint or hire an individual or other person to act on behalf of such entity acting under the authority of law for purposes of collecting any amounts owed.

children.heading: Remedies
children.label: c Remedies
children.text: Remedies available to enforce an affidavit of support under this section include any or all of the remedies described in section 3201, 3203, 3204, or 3205 of title 28, as well as an order for specific performance and payment of legal fees and other costs of collection, and include corresponding remedies available under State law. A Federal agency may seek to collect amounts owed under this section in accordance with the provisions of subchapter II of chapter 37 of title 31.

children.heading: Notification of change of address
children.label: d Notification of change of address

children.heading: General requirement
children.label: 1 General requirement
children.text: The sponsor shall notify the Attorney General and the State in which the sponsored alien is currently a resident within 30 days of any change of address of the sponsor during the period in which an affidavit of support is enforceable.

children.label: A
children.text: not less than $250 or more than $2,000, or

children.label: B
children.text: if such failure occurs with knowledge that the sponsored alien has received any means-tested public benefits (other than benefits described in section 1611(b), 1613(c)(2), or 1621(b) of this title) not less than $2,000 or more than $5,000.
by a sponsored alien, with respect to financial support; or
by the appropriate entity of the Federal Government, a State or any political subdivision of a State, or by any other nongovernmental entity under subsection (b)(2), with respect to reimbursement.

children.label: A
children.text: is a citizen or national of the United States or an alien who is lawfully admitted to the United States for permanent residence;

children.label: B
children.text: is at least 18 years of age;

children.label: C
children.text: is domiciled in any of the several States of the United States, the District of Columbia, or any territory or possession of the United States;

children.label: D
children.text: is petitioning for the admission of the alien under ; andsection 1154 of this title

children.label: E
children.text: demonstrates (as provided in paragraph (6)) the means to maintain an annual income equal to at least 125 percent of the Federal poverty line.

children.heading: Income requirement case
children.label: 2 Income requirement case
children.text: Such term also includes an individual who does not meet the requirement of paragraph (1)(E) but accepts joint and several liability together with an individual under paragraph (5)(A).

children.heading: Active duty armed services case
children.label: 3 Active duty armed services case
children.text: Such term also includes an individual who does not meet the requirement of paragraph (1)(E) but is on active duty (other than active duty for training) in the Armed Forces of the United States, is petitioning for the admission of the alien under as the spouse or child of the individual, and demonstrates (as provided in paragraph (6)) the means to maintain an annual income equal to at least 100 percent of the Federal poverty line.section 1154 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: who does not meet the requirement of paragraph (1)(D), but is the relative of the sponsored alien who filed a classification petition for the sponsored alien as an employment-based immigrant under or who has a significant ownership interest in the entity that filed such a petition; andsection 1153(b) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: who demonstrates (as provided under paragraph (6)) the means to maintain an annual income equal to at least 125 percent of the Federal poverty line, or

children.label: ii
children.text: does not meet the requirement of paragraph (1)(E) but accepts joint and several liability together with an individual under paragraph (5)(A).

children.label: A
children.text: accepts joint and several liability with a petitioning sponsor under paragraph (2) or relative of an employment-based immigrant under paragraph (4) and who demonstrates (as provided under paragraph (6)) the means to maintain an annual income equal to at least 125 percent of the Federal poverty line; or

children.label: i
children.text: the individual petitioning under for the classification of such alien died after the approval of such petition, and the Secretary of Homeland Security has determined for humanitarian reasons that revocation of such petition under would be inappropriate; orsection 1154 of this titlesection 1155 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the alien’s petition is being adjudicated pursuant to section 1154() of this title (surviving relative consideration).l

children.heading: Demonstration of means to maintain income
children.label: 6 Demonstration of means to maintain income

children.heading: Method of demonstration
children.label: i Method of demonstration
children.text: For purposes of this section, a demonstration of the means to maintain income shall include provision of a certified copy of the individual’s Federal income tax return for the individual’s 3 most recent taxable years and a written statement, executed under oath or as permitted under penalty of perjury under , that the copies are certified copies of such returns.section 1746 of title 28

children.heading: Flexibility
children.label: ii Flexibility
children.text: For purposes of this section, aliens may demonstrate the means to maintain income through demonstration of significant assets of the sponsored alien or of the sponsor, if such assets are available for the support of the sponsored alien.

children.heading: Percent of poverty
children.label: iii Percent of poverty
children.text: For purposes of this section, a reference to an annual income equal to at least a particular percentage of the Federal poverty line means an annual income equal to at least such percentage of the Federal poverty line for a family unit of a size equal to the number of members of the sponsor’s household (including family and non-family dependents) plus the total number of other dependents and aliens sponsored by that sponsor.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The Secretary of State, or the Attorney General in the case of adjustment of status, may provide that the demonstration under subparagraph (A) applies only to the most recent taxable year.

children.heading: “Federal poverty line” defined2So in original. Section enacted without a subsec. (g).2
children.label: h “Federal poverty line” defined2So in original. Section enacted without a subsec. (g).2
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “Federal poverty line” means the level of income equal to the official poverty line (as defined by the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, as revised annually by the Secretary of Health and Human Services, in accordance with ) that is applicable to a family of the size involved.section 9902(2) of title 42

children.heading: Sponsor’s social security account number required to be provided
children.label: i Sponsor’s social security account number required to be provided

children.text: An affidavit of support shall include the social security account number of each sponsor.
The Attorney General shall develop an automated system to maintain the social security account number data provided under paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: for the most recent fiscal year for which data are available the number of sponsors under this section and the number of sponsors in compliance with the financial obligations of this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: a comparison of such numbers with the numbers of such sponsors for the preceding fiscal year.
